content.text: Design the game so all interactions, delays, staggers, and transitions are coordinated and in harmony, creating a natural, organized flow. Keep all frontend and backend connections clean, modular, and clearly structured, with easily swappable placeholders so this can serve as a reusable base engine for future arcade video poker variants and mods. Build the architecture thoughtfully now to minimize rewrites later, and keep long-term variant support in mind in every implementation decision.

1вҶ’# Indexed Gameplay Pacing Audit
2вҶ’
3вҶ’Date: 2026-04-05
4вҶ’Source: `C:\Users\Gabi\Documents\GitHub\indexed-lucky5-gameplay`
5вҶ’Input artifacts: `Recording-2026-04-05-112331_compressed_(1).txt`, `ocr.json`, `labels.computervision.json`, `_KeyFrameThumbnail.zip`
6вҶ’
7вҶ’## What This Export Can Reliably Tell Us
8вҶ’
9вҶ’- The capture is `0:04:14.1` long.
10вҶ’- Azure Video Indexer OCR is strong enough to recover cabinet text, brand flashes, control labels, and the moving top-right balance/credit value.
11вҶ’- The export is best for timing and UI-state inference, not exact card-by-card reconstruction.
12вҶ’- A few OCR values are clearly wrong by one digit or one character. Example: `57500` around `0:01:16.8` is almost certainly `157500`.
13вҶ’
14вҶ’## Verified Cabinet Details
15вҶ’
16вҶ’- Portrait layout at `720x1280`.
17вҶ’- Persistent top paytable text:
18вҶ’  `ROYAL FLUSH 2500000`
19вҶ’  `STRAIGHT FLUSH 187500`
20вҶ’  `4 OF A KIND 37500`
21вҶ’  `FULL HOUSE 30000`
22вҶ’  `FLUSH 25000`
23вҶ’  `STRAIGHT 20000`
24вҶ’  `3 OF A KIND 7500`
25вҶ’  `2 PAIR 5000`
26вҶ’- Top-right labels show `CREDIT` and `STAKE`.
27вҶ’- The sampled opening state shows `CREDIT 195000` and `STAKE 2500`.
28вҶ’- Mid-screen prompt is `PRESS HOLDS TO KEEP CARD`.
29вҶ’- Five yellow `HOLD` lamps sit above the control bank.
30вҶ’- Lower controls are always visible and match the clone cabinet layout:
31вҶ’  `BIG`, `SMALL`, `CANCEL HOLD`, `DEAL DRAW`, `BET`, `TAKE HALF`, `MENU`, `TAKE SCORE`.
32вҶ’- The line `4 OF A KIND WINS BONUS` is persistent under the serial/jackpot strip.
33вҶ’
34вҶ’## Pacing Findings
35вҶ’
36вҶ’- The strongest repeatable cadence marker is the OCR span for `PRESS HOLDS TO KEEP CARD`.
37вҶ’- That prompt appears `44` times in `254.1s`, which implies about one playable hand-state every `5.85s` on average.
38вҶ’- The prompt itself stays visible for an average of `1.60s`.
39вҶ’- The shortest prompt window is `0.83s`.
40вҶ’- The longest prompt window is `3.50s`.
41вҶ’- The branded result flash `LUCKY5` appears `43` times.
42вҶ’- `LUCKY5` stays on screen for an average of `0.70s`.
43вҶ’- The branded `POKER` overlay appears `49` times and tracks the same cadence.
44вҶ’- The average delay from `PRESS HOLDS TO KEEP CARD` to the next `LUCKY5` flash is about `4.91s`.
45вҶ’
46вҶ’## Practical Loop Interpretation
47вҶ’
48вҶ’- The clone does not linger on a completed hand.
49вҶ’- The player is given a very short hold-selection window, then the game transitions quickly into a result/brand flash.
50вҶ’- The result flash is brief and acts like punctuation, not a long celebration.
51вҶ’- The cabinet keeps all action-critical controls visible at all times, even when a hand is between states.
52вҶ’- The machine feels fast mostly because the non-interactive windows are short, not because the UI is complex.
53вҶ’
54вҶ’## Credit Animation Behavior
55вҶ’
56вҶ’- The visible credit stream is dominated by `2500`-step changes, which strongly suggests a base stake/bet loop around `2500`.
57вҶ’- Loss-like states usually settle immediately into a new stable balance.
58вҶ’- Win-like states count upward in several fast intermediate steps instead of jumping instantly.
59вҶ’- OCR caught repeated short count-up bursts around:
60вҶ’  `21.333s` to `22.167s`
61вҶ’  `38.667s` to `39.500s`
62вҶ’  `51.200s` to `52.033s`
63вҶ’  `116.267s` to `117.600s`
64вҶ’  `140.000s` to `141.067s`
65вҶ’  `147.200s` to `148.267s`
66вҶ’  `160.267s` to `161.333s`
67вҶ’- The intermediate values are noisy, but the presentation pattern is clear: the clone animates winnings as a rolling meter, not an instant ledger update.
68вҶ’
69вҶ’## Rhythm Changes Over The Clip
70вҶ’
71вҶ’- Early segment: wider gaps between result flashes, including `7s` to `10s` stretches, which reads like setup plus first few hands.
72вҶ’- Core segment: mostly `3s` to `5s` gaps between hold prompt and result punctuation.
73вҶ’- Longer interruptions show up around:
74вҶ’  `108.800s` to `118.667s`
75вҶ’  `134.667s` to `149.600s`
76вҶ’  `188.267s` to `204.033s`
77вҶ’- Those long windows likely correspond to payout count-up, user hesitation, or a slower transition after a notable outcome.
78вҶ’
79вҶ’## What Lucky5 Should Borrow
80вҶ’
81вҶ’- Keep the interaction loop tight: hold-selection prompt visible for roughly `1s` to `2s`, not long enough to feel draggy.
82вҶ’- Keep outcome punctuation short: brand/result flash closer to `0.5s` to `0.8s` than to multi-second celebration.
83вҶ’- Animate wins as short rolling count-ups instead of instant number swaps.
84вҶ’- Preserve always-visible cabinet controls so the player never feels mode-switched out of the machine.
85вҶ’- Preserve the harsh, sparse, almost homemade pacing. The clone feels fast because it is blunt and repetitive.
86вҶ’
87вҶ’## What Lucky5 Should Not Copy Blindly
88вҶ’
89вҶ’- OCR suggests some state transitions are abrupt enough to become unreadable.
90вҶ’- Several hands appear to resolve with almost no breathing room after the result flash.
91вҶ’- The clone likely sacrifices clarity for churn.
92вҶ’- Lucky5 should keep the fast loop, but make result readability and deterministic state sync cleaner than the clone.
93вҶ’
94вҶ’## Confidence And Limits
95вҶ’
96вҶ’- High confidence: cabinet layout, persistent labels, stake size clue, repeated loop cadence, brief brand/result flash behavior, rolling credit-count animation.
97вҶ’- Medium confidence: exact number of hands actually completed, because OCR groups some adjacent states together and misses a few digits.
98вҶ’- Low confidence: exact card-by-card play decisions from this export alone.
99вҶ’
100вҶ’## Bottom Line
101вҶ’
102вҶ’- Yes, the indexed export is enough to understand the clone's pacing.
103вҶ’- The clone's usable rhythm is roughly one hand beat every `5s` to `6s`, with a `1s` to `2s` hold-selection window and a sub-`1s` result flash.
104вҶ’- The strongest upgrade target for Lucky5 is not outcome logic. It is presentation timing: faster hold windows, shorter result punctuation, and a convincing rolling credit animation layered over the clean-room engine.
105вҶ’

2вҶ’

7вҶ’## Summary
8вҶ’
9вҶ’This plan turns the clone-parity brief into an implementation-ready sequence that is faithful to the current Lucky5 repo.
10вҶ’
11вҶ’The goal is to bring Lucky5 to clone-level visual and interaction quality while preserving:
12вҶ’
13вҶ’- the clean-room backend
14вҶ’- the Lebanese cabinet identity
15вҶ’- the current machine-credit and wallet semantics
16вҶ’- the current double-up rules and payout architecture
17вҶ’
18вҶ’This is a **presentation and shell parity** upgrade, not a rules rewrite.
19вҶ’
20вҶ’## Current State Summary
21вҶ’
22вҶ’| Area | Vanilla JS (`server/src/Lucky5.Api/wwwroot/`) | React (`src/web/`) |
23вҶ’|------|-----------------------------------------------|--------------------|
24вҶ’| Shell screens | auth, lobby, wallet, admin, game | auth + in-cabinet machine selection only |
25вҶ’| Cabinet layout | portrait cabinet stack | dashboard-like cabinet plus side telemetry |
26вҶ’| Card rendering | clone PNG assets | CSS-rendered text cards |
27вҶ’| Control deck | clone button art + menu overlay | generic styled controls |
28вҶ’| Jackpot block | close to clone | simplified strip |
29вҶ’| Double-up viewport | trail-in-stage viewport | simplified side panel |
30вҶ’| Menu overlay | present | absent |
31вҶ’| Realtime | SignalR machine sync wired | no equivalent cabinet sync path |

33вҶ’## Execution Stance
34вҶ’
35вҶ’The repo still declares the React cabinet as the active development target, but the vanilla JS cabinet is both the shipped playable surface and the closest clone-faithful implementation.
36вҶ’
37вҶ’The correct execution strategy is:
38вҶ’
39вҶ’1. use the vanilla JS cabinet as the **visual oracle and first parity surface**
40вҶ’2. validate layout, labels, shell flow, and pace there
41вҶ’3. then port the validated structure into the React cabinet as the long-term target
42вҶ’
43вҶ’This is a deliberate sequencing choice, not a change in product direction.
44вҶ’
45вҶ’## Non-Negotiable Guardrails
46вҶ’
47вҶ’Do not change:
48вҶ’
49вҶ’- Lebanese paytable semantics
50вҶ’- Two Pair minimum qualifying hand
51вҶ’- Ace-always-wins double-up behavior
52вҶ’- Lucky 5 switch-only no-lose behavior and multipliers
53вҶ’- deterministic pre-shuffled outcome logic
54вҶ’- backend authority for balances, machine state, jackpots, and double-up outcomes
55вҶ’- retro cabinet visual philosophy
56вҶ’
57вҶ’Do not accidentally redesign:
58вҶ’
59вҶ’- the machine-credit versus wallet model
60вҶ’- jackpot values entering machine credits
61вҶ’- `TAKE SCORE` and `TAKE HALF` settlement behavior
62вҶ’- REST-driven gameplay flow
63вҶ’
64вҶ’SignalR can remain a sync layer for machine state and fanout. Gameplay actions stay REST-driven unless explicitly changed later.
